token_cost.note: Estimated from word count (words * 1.35). Response-only; prompt side not counted.

efficiency.interpretation: With-skill is ~2.9x more verbose per rubric point but wins on correctness/completeness/actionability and ties on structure; clarity is the only criterion where without-skill wins, driven by brevity.

rationale: Consistent +5-point delta on 3 of 4 evals and +4 on the fourth, with the skill strictly winning on the three substantive criteria (correctness, completeness, actionability). The -1 clarity trade-off reflects brevity, not comprehension cost. Token overhead is real but acceptable for the depth gained, especially for design/architecture questions where implementation quality outweighs response length.
